key_insight: The paper derives the first complete analytical vector-geometry model for muon-ring light in dual-mirror Cherenkov telescopes, capturing secondary-mirror and camera shadowing effects that were previously missing.
engineering_relevance: This improves muon-based calibration and optical-throughput monitoring for CTAO dual-mirror designs, especially Schwarzschild-Couder telescopes where geometric losses can shift ring estimates by large amounts.
method: Formulated muon-ring photon geometry with vector algebra for dual-mirror telescope layouts.
Used symbolic manipulation and Taylor expansions in SageMath to derive closed-form expressions.
Validated special cases against known single-mirror solutions and physical limiting behaviors.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports agreement with simpler analytical benchmarks and limiting cases, and finds up to 40% deviations from prior methods in shadowing-dominated configurations.
watch_item: The model is geometry-specific and depends on accurate telescope optics parameters, so implementation must carefully match the actual dual-mirror layout and baffle structure.
paper_title: Using Muon Rings for the Calibration of the Cherenkov Telescope Array: An Analytical Solution for the Dual-Mirror Telescope Using Vector Geometry
